symptoms.symptom_name: Linear coryzal prodrome — sore throat/malaise evolving to clear rhinorrhoea and sneezing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The classic day-by-day coryzal evolution is characteristic of a viral URTI.
A viral prodrome with malaise is not a feature of allergic rhinitis, which lacks a systemic prodrome.

symptoms.symptom_name: Nasal congestion and obstruction with clear mucoid rhinorrhoea

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Turbinate oedema and clear rhinorrhoea are core features of viral URTI.
Congestion and clear rhinorrhoea also occur in allergy — non-discriminating in isolation.
Post-nasal drip and mucosal irritation produce a dry cough in viral URTI.

symptoms.symptom_name: No true dyspnoea at rest — speaking in full sentences, no chest pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of true breathlessness at rest, ability to speak full sentences and absence of chest pain argue strongly against lower-airway involvement despite the alarming 'smothering' language.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of intense nasal or ocular pruritus and no chronic allergic history

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intense itch is the hallmark of allergic rhinitis; its absence plus a viral prodrome argues against allergy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Only low-grade subjective fever, no rigors, no neck stiffness, no rash

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of sudden high fever, rigors, severe myalgia and prostration argues against influenza.
Low-grade fever without systemic toxicity is consistent with a common cold.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clear (non-purulent) nasal discharge, under 10 days, no severe facial pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Purulent discharge, symptoms beyond 10 days and severe localised facial pain are needed to suspect bacterial sinusitis; all are absent.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured: lung fields clear to auscultation, SpO2 99%, low-grade temperature 37.3C, normal vitals

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clear auscultation with normal oxygen saturation excludes a lower respiratory tract complication — a sealed in-person finding the AI Doctor cannot obtain; the AI infers low likelihood from history and normal video appearance, not a measured value.

symptoms.symptom_name: Anterior rhinoscopy: erythematous, markedly swollen nasal turbinates with clear mucoid secretions

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Swollen erythematous turbinates with clear secretions are the mucosal correlate of viral rhinitis — a sealed in-person finding not obtainable via chat.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The linear coryzal prodrome, clear congestion/rhinorrhoea, dry cough and only low-grade fever together form the classic self-limiting viral cold pattern — recognising the gestalt should prevent both antibiotic prescribing and emergency over-escalation.
